Prashanna Raj Pandit
Maryville, IL 62062, USA | +1 (618) 471-8552 | ppandit@siue.edu | prashanna.vercel.app
linkedin.com/in/ra-prashanna | github.com/Prashanna-Raj-Pandit
Summary
Data Scientist and ML Engineer with 2+ years of experience building and deploying production-grade machine learning
systems on AWS cloud infrastructure. Expertise in developing end-to-end ML pipelines leveraging transfer learning,
fine-tuning, and distributed training to achieve significant performance improvements (up to 34% RMSE reduction, 85.6%
classification accuracy). Proven track record in MLOps practices including CI/CD automation, containerization, model
monitoring, and scalable inference deployment. Strong foundation in statistical modeling, deep learning architectures (LSTM,
CNN, Transformer-based embeddings), and cloud-native solutions using AWS SageMaker, Lambda, ECS, S3, and Airflow.

ML/DL Specializations: Deep Learning (LSTM, CNN, Transformer, Embeddings), OpenAI API (LLM integration), NLP,
Computer Vision (OpenPose, MediaPipe, Object Detection), Time-Series Forecasting, Statistical Modeling (Regression, GLM)

Experience
Research Assistant – Machine Learning
Jan 2025 – Present
Southern Illinois University Edwardsville
Edwardsville, IL, USA
– Built a structured ETL pipeline to extract 2D skeletal keypoints from 500,000+ pediatric gait video frames using
OpenPose and MediaPipe; parsed large JSON outputs and transformed pose sequences into 15+ biomechanical features
(stride length, cadence, joint angles, gait cycles) using Python, Pandas, NumPy, and OpenCV.
– Applied advanced signal processing and feature engineering techniques including cubic spline interpolation,
Savitzky–Golay filtering, and gait phase segmentation to time-series data, reducing noise by 40% and enabling
high-quality feature extraction for downstream deep learning models.
– Built production-grade BiLSTM model for autism classification from gait time-series using nestwed cross-validation and
Bayesian hyperparameter optimization (Optuna), achieving 88% mean accuracy; deployed an ensemble of top-performing
models with CI/CD integration, improving recall and reducing false negatives by 25%.
– Deployed scalable ML inference pipeline on AWS using Docker containerization (Amazon ECR), Flask REST APIs hosted

– Deployed scalable ML inference pipeline on AWS using Docker containerization (Amazon ECR), Flask REST APIs hosted
on EC2, and CI/CD automation via GitHub Actions, enabling real-time predictions with 200ms latency and seamless
integration with web applications; implemented CloudWatch monitoring for performance tracking.
Software Engineer – Data & ML Engineering
Jan 2023 – Jul 2024
Vrit Technologies
Kathmandu, Nepal
– Architected and deployed cloud-based Flask web applications with RESTful APIs on AWS EC2, integrating
PostgreSQL/SQLite databases and leveraging S3 for static asset storage; improved application performance by 20%
through query optimization, indexing, and caching strategies.
– Built automated ETL workflows using Python, SQL, and Bash scripting to extract, transform, and load business data
from multiple sources into data warehouses, enabling real-time analytics dashboards and reducing manual data processing
time by 60%.
– Implemented comprehensive CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, Docker, and AWS services, automating testing,
model versioning, and deployment across development/production environments; reduced deployment time by 40% and

model versioning, and deployment across development/production environments; reduced deployment time by 40% and
improved code reliability through automated testing and rollback mechanisms.
– Collaborated with cross-functional teams using Git for version control, conducted code reviews, and maintained API
documentation, improving team productivity by 30% and ensuring best practices in software engineering and MLOps.

Projects
Breast Cancer Classification Using Supervised Machine Learning
Academic Project
– Developed supervised machine learning models to classify breast cancer using routine blood biomarkers and
anthropometric features (age, BMI), enabling non-invasive early risk assessment.
– Evaluated and compared 7 models including Naive Bayes, LDA, KNN, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, SVM, and
Deep Neural Networks using the Breast Cancer Coimbra dataset (116 samples, 9 clinical predictors).
– Applied comprehensive preprocessing including categorical feature encoding and z-score normalization of continuous
variables; engineered derived categorical features to improve model performance.
– Employed stratified train-test split and repeated 10-fold cross-validation to ensure robust and unbiased model evaluation
across imbalanced classes.
– Achieved best performance with Random Forest (Accuracy: 87%, F1-score: 0.88, AUC: 0.91), maintaining a strong
balance between sensitivity (85%) and specificity (90%).
– Performed systematic hyperparameter tuning and model diagnostics including KNN neighbor sweeps and Random Forest
mtry optimization to improve generalization performance.

– Performed systematic hyperparameter tuning and model diagnostics including KNN neighbor sweeps and Random Forest
mtry optimization to improve generalization performance.
– Identified key predictive biomarkers (Glucose, Resistin, Adiponectin), demonstrating that non-invasive clinical indicators
can support early-stage breast cancer screening.
Cloud-Based Time-Series ML Pipeline on AWS | AWS (S3, Glue, SageMaker, Lambda), MLOps
Academic Project
– Built an end-to-end data pipeline on AWS to ingest and store time-series data in Amazon S3 and perform preprocessing
and feature engineering for machine learning.
– Implemented data cleaning and transformation workflows using AWS Glue and Python scripts to prepare training
datasets and ensure data quality.
– Trained machine learning models using Amazon SageMaker notebooks for time-series classification and forecasting tasks,
performing manual hyperparameter tuning and model evaluation.
– Deployed trained models as RESTful inference services using SageMaker endpoints and AWS Lambda for batch
prediction workflows.
– Containerized preprocessing and inference components using Docker and automated model packaging and deployment

prediction workflows.
– Containerized preprocessing and inference components using Docker and automated model packaging and deployment
using GitHub Actions and Amazon ECR.
– Monitored pipeline execution and inference performance using Amazon CloudWatch logs and basic metrics to ensure
system reliability.
MedNLPify: Medical Abstract Classifier with Transfer Learning | NLP, Transfer Learning
Academic Project
– Designed and trained state-of-the-art Tribrid Deep Learning architecture leveraging transfer learning from Universal
Sentence Encoder (TensorFlow Hub) combined with character-level CNNs and positional embeddings to classify 200,000+
PubMed RCT sentences into 5 categories.
– Fine-tuned pre-trained embeddings and optimized architecture through systematic experimentation, improving accuracy
from baseline 72.5% to 85.6% (18% relative improvement) and achieving F1-score of 0.84; applied regularization (dropout,
L2), batch normalization, and learning rate scheduling.
– Built end-to-end ML pipeline including data preprocessing (sentence tokenization with spaCy), transfer learning model

L2), batch normalization, and learning rate scheduling.
– Built end-to-end ML pipeline including data preprocessing (sentence tokenization with spaCy), transfer learning model
development, evaluation (confusion matrix, ROC curves), and deployment as Flask REST API; containerized with Docker
for portability across environments.
– Implemented MLOps best practices with CI/CD using GitHub Actions for automated testing, model versioning, and
deployment; reduced manual build time by 45% and deployed Chrome extension for real-time medical text classification.
Predictive Analytics on Iranian Telecom Data | R, Statistical Modeling, ML, Tableau
Academic Project
– Developed advanced statistical models (Poisson, Negative Binomial, Ridge, Lasso regression) to forecast telecom network
usage patterns on 10,000+ customer records, addressing overdispersion and multicollinearity; achieved 34% RMSE
reduction through regularization and feature selection.
– Built binary classification model using Logistic Regression with SMOTE for imbalanced customer complaint prediction,
improving AUC from 0.82 to 0.924 (12% lift) and accuracy to 87.5%; identified top 5 predictive features contributing to

improving AUC from 0.82 to 0.924 (12% lift) and accuracy to 87.5%; identified top 5 predictive features contributing to
70% of variance using feature importance analysis.
– Performed comprehensive EDA using R (dplyr, ggplot2), engineered interaction and polynomial features, and validated
models using k-fold cross-validation and holdout testing to ensure robust generalization; documented statistical
assumptions and model diagnostics.
– Created interactive Tableau dashboards visualizing customer churn patterns, usage frequency distributions, tariff plan
analysis, and KPIs (retention rate, ARPU), enabling data-driven decision-making for business stakeholders.
Publications & Awards
Best Paper Award – ICT-CEEL 2023 (International Conference on Technologies for Computer, Electrical, Electronics &
Communication): Awarded for the paper ”Automation of Driving License Test using Computer Vision and Image Processing”,
an AI-based system leveraging transfer learning from YOLO object detection models, achieving 95% mAP for automated
driving-test evaluation. View on ResearchGate

build end-to-end machine learning pipelines. My work spans the
full lifecycle: from data analysis and feature engineering to model
development, evaluation, and deployment.

Planet Pharma, 
Dear Hiring Manager, 
I am writing to express my interest in the Data Scientist position. I am currently pursuing my 
Master’s in Computer Science at Southern Illinois University Edwardsville and working as a 
Research Assistant, where I design and deploy machine learning pipelines for healthcare-focused 
applications. My background in statistical modeling, machine learning, and end-to-end data 
pipelines aligns well with the responsibilities of this role, particularly in developing analytical 
solutions to support clinical research and data-driven decision-making. 
In my current role, I work with noisy medical video and time-series data, where I design data 
preprocessing pipelines, perform feature engineering, and build predictive models using Python, 
TensorFlow, and Scikit-learn. I have developed and validated deep learning models for autism 
detection using gait analysis, with a strong focus on data quality, model robustness, and 
reproducibility. This experience has strengthened my ability to translate real-world clinical 
problems into well-defined analytical tasks and deploy solutions that can be monitored and 
improved in production environments.

problems into well-defined analytical tasks and deploy solutions that can be monitored and 
improved in production environments. 
I also bring hands-on experience with building scalable ML workflows, including ETL pipelines, 
model packaging with Docker, CI/CD automation, and deploying RESTful APIs for inference. 
Beyond technical skills, I regularly collaborate with faculty researchers and cross-functional 
stakeholders to define analytical objectives, communicate results clearly, and ensure that insights 
are actionable. I enjoy working in multidisciplinary environments and contributing as an analytics 
consultant to help guide decisions with evidence-based insights. 
What excites me most about this opportunity is the chance to apply advanced analytics and 
machine learning in a real-world, regulated setting where high-quality data science directly 
supports impactful outcomes. I am particularly motivated by roles that combine statistical rigor, 
machine learning innovation, and collaboration across teams to solve meaningful problems. 
I would welcome the opportunity to contribute my skills, curiosity, and strong work ethic to your

machine learning innovation, and collaboration across teams to solve meaningful problems. 
I would welcome the opportunity to contribute my skills, curiosity, and strong work ethic to your 
team. Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the possibility of discussing 
how my background and interests align with your goals. 
Sincerely, 
Prashanna Raj Pandit
